title: CI gates: readability, accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA), staleness fail-safe (validUntil)
context: Quality CI gates for ewing-survivorship-late-effects ensuring patient-facing pages stay readable, accessible, and current. They enforce the plain-language target and accessibility commitment and wire the validUntil staleness fail-safe from the schema/policy spec (001) so claims tied to a superseded guideline version are auto-flagged. These complement the safety/licensing gates (003) but do not replace clinical/advocate review.
objective: Implement readability (grade 6-8), accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA), and staleness (validUntil) CI gates with fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: Readability gate measures reading grade and flags core pages outside the grade 6-8 target
Accessibility gate checks pages against WCAG 2.2 AA and fails on violations
Staleness fail-safe: an assertion past its guideline version's validUntil is auto-flagged/withheld until re-verified and re-dual-signed-off
All gates exercised by passing and failing fixtures in CI
output: packages/gates plus CI wiring: readability, accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA), and staleness (validUntil) checks with fixtures
